# SENDEX — Full Reference for AI Consumers

> Deep index of SENDEX public documentation. Inlined method IDs and section
> outlines so an agent can locate and cite specifics without scanning every
> file.

SENDEX (SEND Explorer) analyzes pre-clinical regulatory study data in
CDISC SENDIG 3.1 format. XPT inputs → Python generator pipeline →
React/TypeScript viewer. Output is structured JSON under
`backend/generated/<study>/`. Every method, threshold, and rule below
is auto-generated from live code and stays in sync with the engine.

## Table of contents

1. Methods registry — stable IDs (STAT, METH, CLASS, DATA)
2. Scientific logic — syndromes (XS01-XS10, XC01-XC12) and behavior (B.1-B.6)
3. Validation — suite design, study cards, signal detection
4. Conventions, constraints, and pitfalls

---

## 1. Methods registry ([methods.md](methods.md))

Organized by category. Each ID is the stable reference used in specs
(via frontmatter `methods:` fields) and in backend/frontend code comments.

### Statistical tests (STAT-*)

| ID | Name |
|----|------|
| STAT-01 | Welch's t-test (two-tailed, unequal variance) |
| STAT-02 | Mann-Whitney U |
| STAT-03 | Boschloo's exact test (2x2) — default; Fisher's exact as override |
| STAT-04 | Jonckheere-Terpstra trend test |
| STAT-05 | Cochran-Armitage trend test |
| STAT-06 | One-way ANOVA F-test |
| STAT-07 | Dunnett's test |
| STAT-08 | Kruskal-Wallis H-test |
| STAT-09 | Spearman rank correlation |
| STAT-10 | Bonferroni correction |
| STAT-11 | Binomial SE tolerance |
| STAT-12 | Hedges' g effect size (bias-corrected) |
| STAT-12b | Cohen's d effect size (uncorrected) |
| STAT-12c | Glass's Δ effect size (control SD only) |
| STAT-13 | Welch pairwise (raw p-values for Bonferroni) |
| STAT-14 | Williams' step-down test (pairwise) |
| STAT-14b | Williams' trend test |
| STAT-15 | ANCOVA (analysis of covariance) |

### Methods (METH-*)

| ID | Name |
|----|------|
| METH-01 | Dual-pass early-death exclusion |
| METH-02 | BW percent change from baseline |
| METH-03 | Relative organ weight (organ-to-body ratio) |
| METH-03a | Organ weight normalization auto-selection (Phase 1) |
| METH-04 | MI severity score mapping |
| METH-05 | Incidence normalization |
| METH-06 | Tissue/biomarker organ-system resolution |
| METH-07 | Tumor morphology to cell type |
| METH-08 | SETCD to dose-level mapping (TK satellite) |
| METH-08a | TK satellite detection and statistical exclusion |
| METH-09 | ISO 8601 duration parsing |
| METH-10 | BQL handling (LLOQ/2 imputation) |
| METH-11 | TK survivorship cross-reference |
| METH-12 | Rule suppression (deduplication) |
| METH-13 | Direction determination |
| METH-14 | Cross-domain syndrome detection (XS01–XS10, XC01a–XC12c) |
| METH-15 | Compound expression evaluator |
| METH-16 | Endpoint synonym resolution |
| METH-17 | Endpoint aggregation (multi-row dedup) |
| METH-18 | Organ coherence derivation (evidence_score composition) |
| METH-19 | Histopathology proxy matching |
| METH-20 | Sex-divergence projection |
| METH-21 | MedDRA dictionary key building |
| METH-22 | Discriminator evaluation (two-pass) |
| METH-23 | Finding term normalization |
| METH-24 | Stress confound detection (REM-10) |
| METH-25 | Adaptive response pattern (REM-16) |
| METH-26 | Species-specific preferred biomarkers (REM-11) |
| METH-27 | Treatment-relatedness reasoning trace (REM-17) |
| METH-28 | Histopathologic severity grade extraction (REM-21) |
| METH-29 | Data sufficiency gate (REM-15 + REM-20) |
| METH-30 | Magnitude floor per endpoint class (REM-27) |
| METH-31 | Certainty upgrade evidence (v0.3.0 PATCH-04) |
| METH-32 | Food consumption per-sex key stats derivation |
| METH-38 | Cross-organ chain detection |

### Classifications (CLASS-*)

| ID | Name |
|----|------|
| CLASS-01 | Severity classification |
| CLASS-02 | Dose-response pattern (continuous) |
| CLASS-03 | Dose-response pattern (incidence/histopath) |
| CLASS-04 | Pattern confidence scoring |
| CLASS-05 | Treatment-relatedness determination |
| CLASS-06 | NOAEL/LOAEL derivation (backend) |
| CLASS-07 | Endpoint NOAEL derivation (frontend, pairwise) |
| CLASS-08 | Target organ flagging |
| CLASS-09 | Cross-domain syndrome confidence |
| CLASS-10 | Recovery verdict |
| CLASS-10b | Recovery verdict (continuous endpoints) |
| CLASS-11 | Protective signal classification |
| CLASS-12 | Syndrome certainty assessment |
| CLASS-13 | Adversity assessment (ECETOC B-factors) |
| CLASS-14 | Overall severity cascade |
| CLASS-15 | Lab rule severity grading (L01–L31) |
| CLASS-16 | Endpoint confidence (findings rail) |
| CLASS-17 | Dose-proportionality (log-log PK) |
| CLASS-18 | Rule engine (R01–R19) |
| CLASS-19 | Finding nature classification |
| CLASS-20 | Recovery classification (interpretive layer) |
| CLASS-21 | Organ proportionality index (OPI) classification |
| CLASS-22 | Non-monotonic detection |
| CLASS-23 | Trend test validity |
| CLASS-24 | Normalization confidence ceiling |
| CLASS-25 | Integrated endpoint confidence |
| CLASS-26 | NOAEL contribution weight |
| CLASS-27 | Weighted NOAEL derivation |

### Data handling (DATA-*)

| ID | Name |
|----|------|
| DATA-01 | Recovery-animal treatment-period pooling |
| DATA-02 | Time-course y-axis transformations |

---

## 2. Scientific logic ([scientific-logic.md](scientific-logic.md))

Auto-generated review doc showing the full pipeline applied to
PointCross (RAT, Sprague-Dawley, oral gavage, 13-week subchronic).
61 endpoint summaries → 10 detected syndromes.

### Syndromes — cross-domain (XS01–XS10)

| ID | Syndrome |
|----|----------|
| XS01 | Hepatocellular injury |
| XS02 | Hepatobiliary / cholestatic |
| XS03 | Nephrotoxicity |
| XS04 | Myelosuppression |
| XS05 | Hemolytic anemia |
| XS06 | Phospholipidosis |
| XS07 | Immunotoxicity |
| XS08 | Stress response |
| XS09 | Target organ wasting |
| XS10 | Cardiovascular |

### Syndromes — organ-specific (XC01–XC12)

| ID | Syndrome |
|----|----------|
| XC01a/b/c | BM myeloid / erythroid / megakaryocyte suppression |
| XC02 | Hemolytic anemia multi-organ |
| XC03a/b | Thyroid FCHH — enzyme induction / direct |
| XC04a/b/c | Adrenal cortical hypertrophy (stress) / atrophy (HPA suppression) / vacuolation (steroidogenesis block) |
| XC05 | Adrenal medullary effects |
| XC06a/b/c | Testicular germ cell toxicity / Leydig cell / male accessory sex organ atrophy |
| XC07a | Ovarian follicular effects |
| XC08a/b | Uterine atrophy / hyperplasia |
| XC09 | CNS neuronal degeneration |
| XC10 | PNS axonal degeneration |
| XC11a/b | Dermal / injection-site effects |
| XC12a/b/c | Retinal / lens / corneal effects |

### Interpretive behavior (B.1–B.6)

| Section | Topic |
|---------|-------|
| B.1 | Certainty assessment |
| B.2 | Treatment-relatedness (ECETOC A-factors) |
| B.3 | Adversity assessment (ECETOC B-factors) |
| B.4 | Severity cascade |
| B.5 | Translational confidence |
| B.6 | Statistical methods (REM-06) |

---

## 3. Validation ([validation/](validation/))

Two-layer validation suite:

- **Engine output** — per-study facts read directly from generated JSON. No verdicts, no human input.
- **Signal detection** — binary DETECTED or MISSED verdicts against reference cards. Design assertions use MATCH or MISMATCH.

Most studies are **synthetic datasets** with known, deliberately injected signals.

### Key files

- [summary.md](validation/summary.md): Dashboard table — per-study signal score, design match, assertion outcomes
- [engine-output.md](validation/engine-output.md): Per-study facts (design, dose groups, classification counts, NOAEL/LOAEL, target organs, provenance)
- [signal-detection.md](validation/signal-detection.md): Per-signal DETECTED/MISSED verdicts with effect sizes
- [README.md](validation/README.md): Suite design and study sources

### Reference cards ([validation/references/](validation/references/))

One YAML per study, encoding injected signals and expected design. Every claim traces to nSDRG, study report, or define.pdf.

Current coverage:
- CBER-POC-Pilot-Study1 through Study5 (synthetic vaccine / gene-therapy)
- CJ16050-xptonly (synthetic rat)
- CJUGSEND00 (synthetic)
- FFU-Contribution-to-FDA (real)
- instem, Nimble, PDS (synthetic)
- PointCross (synthetic rat — the reference study for scientific-logic.md)
- TOXSCI-35449 dog Compound B (real)
- TOXSCI-43066 dog Compound A (real)
- TOXSCI-87497 rat Compound B (real)
- TOXSCI-96298 rat Compound A (real)

---

## 4. Conventions, constraints, and pitfalls

### Small-N preclinical

SENDEX targets **non-rodent N=3–5** as well as rodent N=10. Advice
assuming N>30 or parametric-normality defaults is wrong for this
domain. The engine prefers robust / non-parametric methods.

The only load-bearing normality assumption is tracked as GAP-236 (HCD
reference ranges use `mean ± 2*sd` which understates the upper tail
for log-normal endpoints like ALT, AST, creatinine, bilirubin, GGT).

### SEND domain codes

Standard per CDISC SENDIG 3.1:

- DM — demographics
- EX — exposure
- LB — laboratory test results
- BW — body weights
- MI — microscopic findings (histopathology)
- MA — macroscopic findings
- CL — clinical observations
- OM — organ measurements
- TK — toxicokinetics
- TS — trial summary
- PC — pharmacokinetic concentrations
- FW — food / water consumption
- BG — body-gain / growth-related
- VS — vital signs
- TF — tumor findings

### Dose groups

Labeled by ascending dose level; control is dose 0. High dose = last
index. TK satellite animals handled separately (METH-08, METH-08a).

### Primary regulatory outputs

- **Target organ identification** — organs with convergent evidence across domains (MI, OM, LB)
- **Dose-response patterns** — continuous (CLASS-02) and incidence (CLASS-03)
- **NOAEL / LOAEL** — backend derivation (CLASS-06) + frontend pairwise view (CLASS-07)
- **Reversibility verdicts** — recovery (CLASS-10 / CLASS-10b / CLASS-20)
- **Treatment-relatedness** — with reasoning trace (CLASS-05, METH-27)

### Statistical conservatism

- Pre-specified test assignment (not data-adaptive)
- Robust effect sizes (Hedges' g, MAD-based z-scores)
- Bonferroni correction for multi-comparisons (STAT-10)
- Williams' step-down as trend alternative (STAT-14)
- LOO (leave-one-out) stability for fragile NOAELs

### Species coverage

Rat, dog, rabbit, NHP (cynomolgus), guinea pig. Mouse coverage is
partial — see GAP-262 (dedicated mouse syndrome override profile).

### Key fields in generated JSON

- `unified_findings.json` — canonical finding list per study
- `noael_summary.json` — NOAEL/LOAEL derivation
- `target_organ_summary.json` — target organs with evidence scoring
- `group_stats[]` on each finding — per-dose statistics
- `pairwise[]` on each finding — pairwise vs control
- `group_stats[].severity_grade_counts` — histopathology severity bins

See `docs/_internal/knowledge/field-contracts-index.md` (private) for
full field definitions.

---

## Regeneration

- `docs/methods.md` — regenerated from code annotations by the methods-generator
- `docs/scientific-logic.md` — regenerated via `/regen-science` against a fixed reference study (PointCross)
- `docs/validation/*.md` — regenerated via `/regen-validation`

This file (`llms-full.txt`) is currently curated, not auto-generated.
Regeneration cadence: update when method IDs, syndrome IDs, or
validation study coverage change. Tracked as follow-up to GAP-265.
